summary: Soundboard recording of good quality captures a short but R&B‑rich 1969 set highlighted by a fiery “New Orleans” and “Casey Jones”.
review: The three fan reviews are uniformly enthusiastic, calling the show a must‑have for collectors and praising the Pigpen‑driven R&B vibe. Standout moments include a “funky Casey Jones” opener, an electrifying “New Orleans”, and a long, tasty “Lovelight”. The soundboard source is generally clear, though a few reviewers note low‑bitrate MP3 artifacts and suggest using EQ for optimal playback.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are strongly positive about both performance and sound; recording is good but mentions of MP3 quality keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear overall mix praised as "great sound"; a couple of comments about low‑bitrate MP3s and recommendation to use EQ indicate minor fidelity issues but no major dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A concise ten‑song set that leans heavily into R&B thanks to Pigpen, delivering a tight, high‑energy flow with memorable transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia embraces the R&B feel, even singing “Good Lovin’” and adding soulful guitar tone.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ/keyboard work is highlighted as the driving R&B force behind the performance.
